chunk_summary: This section outlines a stepwise integrated escalation algorithm for managing feeding and respiratory challenges in children with neurodisability, emphasizing conservative failure criteria and individualized care plans based on NICE and ESPGHAN guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the stepwise approach to managing feeding and respiratory challenges in children with neurodisability.
Learn to classify baseline function using GMFCS and EDACS tools and identify red flags for escalation.
Recognize conservative failure criteria and appropriate escalation to non-oral feeding methods.
Apply NICE and ESPGHAN guidelines to create individualized feeding plans.
key_facts: Baseline function should be classified using GMFCS and EDACS (or MINI-EDACS for ages 18-36 months).
Conservative failure criteria include ongoing cough, choking, weight decline, dehydration, or mealtime durations overwhelming caregivers, reviewed within 2-6 weeks.
Inpatient evaluation is necessary for suspected pneumonia requiring oxygen, unsafe hydration, or caregiver inability to execute plans.
Texture modification should follow posture, bolus pacing, and utensil adjustments, per NICE guidance.
Non-oral feeding escalation should begin with short-term NG/NJ trials before considering gastrostomy for long-term support.
reference_quote: NICE Guideline NG62 recommends individualized feeding plans for children with cerebral palsy, emphasizing texture modification and caregiver support.
document_key_topics: Integrated feeding and respiratory management in neurodisability
Conservative failure criteria for feeding plans
Stepwise escalation to non-oral feeding methods
Caregiver burden and follow-up in feeding support
